title: Crispy Malaysian Desiccated Coconut Stir-fry
description: A light and crispy dish featuring desiccated coconut in a savory sauce that is perfect for lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: Desiccated Coconut
ingredients.notes: Grated coconut can be used as a substitute if unavailable.

ingredients.name: Soaked Cashews (or Almonds)
ingredients.notes: Soaked overnight or for at least 4 hours to soften.

ingredients.name: Chicken Breasts (optional)
ingredients.notes: Bone-in, skin-on for added flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce (or Tamari)
ingredients.notes: For umami flavor and to balance the dish.

ingredients.name: Kecap Manis (optional)
ingredients.notes: Indonesian sweet soy sauce, adds a touch of sweetness to complement the coconut's natural sweetness.

ingredients.name: Pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground pepper for more authenticity.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil or Coconut Oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing and to create a crispy exterior on the dish.

instructions: Prepare your ingredients by soaking cashews (if using) overnight, then grate desiccated coconut.
In a wok or large frying pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add chicken pieces skin-side down if using.
Once golden on the bottom, flip to brown on all sides, about 5 minutes per side, and remove from heat.
To the same pan, add chopped garlic and shallots; sauté for another minute until fragrant but not burnt.
Add grated coconut and cashews; stir-fry for a minute, allowing the coconut to release its moisture slightly.
Pour in vegetable broth or water, and let it come to a simmer. Add soy sauce (or tamari) and kecap manis (if using). Stir well.
Return chicken pieces to pan if not already added, and continue to cook until liquid is absorbed and dish thickens slightly, about 5-7 minutes more.
Stir occasionally to ensure even cooking and that the coconut doesn't burn on bottom of wok. Season with freshly ground pepper as desired.
